etymology.description: Pictogram of bow shooting an arrow -> 攴 (hand) added, forming 㢭 -> phonetic 癹 added -> final form 發
etymology.interpretation: The character originally depicted a bow shooting an arrow, symbolizing the idea of sending forth or releasing. The addition of the hand component indicates an action, while the phonetic 癹 contributes to the pronunciation.
etymology.simplification: 發 was simplified to 发 by removing the hand component 攴 and replacing 癹 with the grass radical 夂, retaining a similar sound.

examples.chinese: 老师发了作业(老師發了作業)。
他生气时就会发火(他生氣時就會發火)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shēngqì shí jiù huì fāhuǒ.
examples.english: He emits anger when he gets mad.

examples.chinese: 公司发了奖金(公司發了獎金)，员工都很高兴(員工都很高興)。
examples.pinyin: Gōngsī fā le jiǎngjīn, yuángōng dōu hěn gāoxìng.
examples.english: The company issued a bonus, and all the employees were very happy.
